# Generated by ollama (qwen3-coder:30b)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { Application, Router } from "https://deno.land/x/oak@v12.1.0/mod.ts";

interface User {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  email: string;
  age: number;
  department: string;
}

const kv = await Deno.openKv();

const router = new Router();

// Create or update user
router.put("/users/:id", async (ctx) => {
  if (!ctx.params?.id) {
    ctx.response.status = 400;
    ctx.response.body = { error: "User ID is required" };
    return;
  }

  const user = await ctx.request.body({ type: "json" }).value;
  user.id = ctx.params.id;

  await kv.set(["users", user.id], user);
  await kv.set(["users", "index", user.department, user.id], user);
  await kv.set(["users", "index", "age", user.age.toString(), user.id], user);

  ctx.response.body = { message: "User created/updated successfully" };
});

// Search users with flexible filters
router.get("/users", async (ctx) => {
  const queryParams = ctx.request.url.searchParams;
  const filters: Record<string, string> = {};

  for (const [key, value] of queryParams.entries()) {
    if (key !== "limit" && key !== "offset") {
      filters[key] = value;
    }
  }

  const limit = parseInt(queryParams.get("limit") || "100");
  const offset = parseInt(queryParams.get("offset") || "0");

  let keys: Array<string | number> = ["users"];

  if (Object.keys(filters).length > 0) {
    const filterKeys = Object.keys(filters);
    const firstFilter = filterKeys[0];
    const firstFilterValue = filters[firstFilter];
    
    if (firstFilter === "id") {
      keys = ["users", firstFilterValue];
    } else {
      keys = ["users", "index", firstFilter, firstFilterValue];
    }
  }

  const result: User[] = [];
  const iter = kv.list<User>({ prefix: keys }, { limit, offset });

  for await (const { key, value } of iter) {
    if (key.length > 2) {
      const user = value;
      let matches = true;
      
      for (const [filterKey, filterValue] of Object.entries(filters)) {
        if (user[filterKey as keyof User] !== filterValue) {
          matches = false;
          break;
        }
      }
      
      if (matches) {
        result.push(user);
      }
    }
  }

  ctx.response.body = result;
});

// Get user by ID
router.get("/users/:id", async (ctx) => {
  if (!ctx.params?.id) {
    ctx.response.status = 400;
    ctx.response.body = { error: "User ID is required" };
    return;
  }

  const { value } = await kv.get<User>(["users", ctx.params.id]);
  
  if (!value) {
    ctx.response.status = 404;
    ctx.response.body = { error: "User not found" };
    return;
  }

  ctx.response.body = value;
});

// Delete user
router.delete("/users/:id", async (ctx) => {
  if (!ctx.params?.id) {
    ctx.response.status = 400;
    ctx.response.body = { error: "User ID is required" };
    return;
  }

  const { value } = await kv.get<User>(["users", ctx.params.id]);
  
  if (!value) {
    ctx.response.status = 404;
    ctx.response.body = { error: "User not found" };
    return;
  }

  await kv.delete(["users", ctx.params.id]);
  await kv.delete(["users", "index", value.department, ctx.params.id]);
  await kv.delete(["users", "index", "age", value.age.toString(), ctx.params.id]);

  ctx.response.body = { message: "User deleted successfully" };
});

const app = new Application();
app.use(router.routes());
app.use(router.allowedMethods());

await app.listen({ port: 8000 });
